is an English [sports club](/wiki/Sports_club "Sports club") based in [Kingsland, Herefordshire](/wiki/Kingsland,_Herefordshire "Kingsland, Herefordshire"). The [rugby union](/wiki/Rugby_union "Rugby union") team currently plays in [National League 2 West](/wiki/National_League_2_West "National League 2 West"), the fourth tier of the [English rugby union system](/wiki/English_rugby_union_system "English rugby union system"), following their promotion from the [Midlands Premier](/wiki/Midlands_Premier "Midlands Premier") at the end of the 2018–19 season via the play-offs. The club runs four senior sides and a full range of junior sides. Apart from rugby, Luctonians hosts the practice of [cricket](/wiki/Cricket "Cricket"), [golf](/wiki/Golf "Golf"), [cycling](/wiki/Cycle_sport "Cycle sport"), having also a [netball](/wiki/Netball "Netball"), rounders, ladies touch and ladies contact section for women.[[1]](#cite_note-1)
Not to be confused with [Midlands 1 West](/wiki/Midlands_1_West "Midlands 1 West").
["NM CUP PREVIOUS WINNERS"](http://www.northmidsrfu.co.uk/north-midlands-cup-competitions/north-midlands-county-cup-winners/). North Midlands RFU. Retrieved 11 May 2016.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

English rugby union club, based in Herefordshire

Rugby team

Luctonians

**Luctonians Sports Club** is an English [sports club](/wiki/Sports_club "Sports club") based in [Kingsland, Herefordshire](/wiki/Kingsland,_Herefordshire "Kingsland, Herefordshire"). The [rugby union](/wiki/Rugby_union "Rugby union") team currently plays in [National League 2 West](/wiki/National_League_2_West "National League 2 West"), the fourth tier of the [English rugby union system](/wiki/English_rugby_union_system "English rugby union system"), following their promotion from the [Midlands Premier](/wiki/Midlands_Premier "Midlands Premier") at the end of the 2018–19 season via the play-offs. The club runs four senior sides and a full range of junior sides. Apart from rugby, Luctonians hosts the practice of [cricket](/wiki/Cricket "Cricket"), [golf](/wiki/Golf "Golf"), [cycling](/wiki/Cycle_sport "Cycle sport"), having also a [netball](/wiki/Netball "Netball"), rounders, ladies touch and ladies contact section for women.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Ground

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Luctonians&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Ground")]

Luctonians play home games at Mortimer Park on Hereford Road in the village of [Kingsland](/wiki/Kingsland,_Herefordshire "Kingsland, Herefordshire") – about 4 miles from the nearest town, [Leominster](/wiki/Leominster "Leominster").[[2]](#cite_note-2) Ground capacity is around 2,500 – a figure that includes 300 seated in the modern grandstand (completed in 2013) – with the rest standing space.[[3]](#cite_note-3) Due to its rural location the only access to Mortimer Park is by car, although there is ample parking space.

On 27 April 2019, Luctonians achieved a record attendance of 1,757, who watched them defeat [Blaydon](/wiki/Blaydon_RFC "Blaydon RFC") in the Midlands-North play-off, to achieve promotion to [National League 2 North](/wiki/National_League_2_North "National League 2 North").

## Honours

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Luctonians&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Honours")]

* [North Midlands 1](/wiki/North_Midlands_1 "North Midlands 1") champions: 1993–94
* [Midlands West 2](/wiki/Midlands_3_West_(South) "Midlands 3 West (South)") champions: 1994–95
* [Midlands West 1](/wiki/Midlands_2_West_(South) "Midlands 2 West (South)")[[a]](#cite_note-4) champions: 1995–96
* [Midlands 2 (East v West)](/wiki/Midlands_1_West#Promotion_play-offs "Midlands 1 West") promotion play-off winners: 2000–01
* [North Midlands Cup](/wiki/North_Midlands_Cup "North Midlands Cup") winners (4): 2004–05, 2005–06, 2007–08, 2008–09[[4]](#cite_note-nmcupwinners-5)
* [Midlands Premier v North Premier](/wiki/Midlands_Premier#Promotion_play-offs "Midlands Premier") promotion play-off winners (2): 2009–10, 2018–19

Rules for classification: If teams are level at any stage, tiebreakers are applied in the following order:

1. Number of matches won
2. Number of matches drawn
3. Difference between points for and against
4. Total number of points for
5. Aggregate number of points scored in matches between tied teams
6. Number of matches won excluding the first match, then the second and so on until the tie is settled
